body.title: CLOSED: InferenceDevice dispatch + bus envelopes (Stage 1)
body.result: Stage 1: InferenceDevice.dispatch + InferenceRequest/InferenceResponse envelope types; 16 tests; pushed to agent_datacenter
body.description:
## RE-SIZE + STAGED PLAN 2026-05-03 (CC surfaced before sprinting)

**Re-size: M → L.** Investigation while claiming this ticket showed the actual scope is bigger than the M estimate:
- agent_datacenter inference device today is registration/health-only (183 lines, no dispatch)
- Adding real dispatch means extracting the OR client + ollama HTTP client logic from wild_igor/igor/cognition/inference_openrouter.py into agent_datacenter
- Then migrating Igor call sites (cognition/local_preparse.py, cognition/consult.py, main.py, boot_check.py)
- inference_openrouter.py is HIGH-inertia (cognition path); editing it during the autonomous queue-clear phase risks Igor's primary inference. Per slate strategic shift: "we stop trying to make Igor self-code right now and instead clear everything else first" — leaving Igor cognition path edits for the post-queue-clear work.

**Suggested staged migration (Akien call):**

- **Stage 1** (M): in agent_datacenter, extract the OR + ollama client logic from inference_openrouter into a new shared module the InferenceDevice owns. InferenceDevice gains `dispatch(InferenceRequest) -> InferenceResponse`. Tests cover both modes (mocked HTTP). Igor's path unchanged — both paths exist side-by-side.
- **Stage 2** (M): migrate Igor call sites one-at-a-time to use the datacenter dispatch via `comms://inference` envelopes through the announce/manifest path. Each migration is its own commit; test-and-validate before the next.
- **Stage 3** (S): delete the duplicated logic in inference_openrouter.py once all sites are migrated. Inference_openrouter becomes a thin compat shim or is deleted.

**Why surfacing not sprinting:** L+HIGH-inertia + sequential cutover risk to Igor's cognition path. CC's autonomous-sprint stop condition is "surface real design blocks instead of guessing." This is one — Akien picks when stage 1 sprints, and whether to chunk further.

**No code changed in this claim cycle.** Ticket reset to pending.

---

## Status 2026-05-03 — READY TO SPRINT (P1)
Akien clearance: this ticket is well-scoped as written; no further design input needed. CC overclassified it as "needs design judgment" because it touches Igor inference; it does not — the architectural North Star already settled the shape. P1 as a child of T-capability-extraction-from-igor (also P1).

---

Per Akien architectural North Star (2026-05-01): the datacenter is the agent-as-pieces (capability appliances), Igor is the master reasoning model. Inference is a capability appliance — it should live in the datacenter, not in Igor's tree. Phase 2-3 of agent_datacenter shipped an `InferenceDevice` (commit 0669cb5) but it's currently a registration/health shell — its docstring says 'The device does not own the inference connection — it provides health reporting and comms:// registration so other devices can dispatch via the bus.' Meanwhile Igor's actual inference still lives at `wild_igor/igor/cognition/inference_openrouter.py` with no `agent_datacenter` imports anywhere in `wild_igor/igor/`. This ticket closes that gap.

**Affected files:** `/home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/devices/inference/device.py` (extend to actually own dispatch — accept inference envelopes, route to OR/Ollama based on INFERENCE_MODE); `/home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/devices/inference/shim.py` (envelope shape for bus clients); `wild_igor/igor/cognition/inference_openrouter.py` (thin to a bus client that emits `comms://inference` envelopes; or delete if everything migrates cleanly); call sites of inference inside Igor (cognition/local_preparse.py, cognition/consult.py, main.py, boot_check.py — verify each).

**Design rules:** docs-in-code (inference device + shim are load-bearing — refresh top-of-file docstrings); test-plan-or-why-not; budget-respect (route preserves existing OR vs Ollama selection logic); no-secrets-in-repo (OR API key stays in env).

**Scope boundary:** IN — datacenter inference device dispatches actual inference calls; Igor calls inference via the bus; existing INFERENCE_MODE/INFERENCE_ENDPOINT envs preserved; OR + Ollama both work via the device. OUT — load-balancing across multiple inference endpoints (later); inference cache layer (later); changing the prompt-shape contracts (purely a transport migration).

**Test plan:** `tests/test_inference_via_bus.py` in agent_datacenter — assert envelope sent to comms://inference produces a valid completion (mocked OR + mocked Ollama, both modes). Igor-side: `tests/test_igor_inference_bus_client.py` — assert the bus client emits the correct envelope shape and consumes the response. Integration smoke test: a real Igor turn end-to-end with inference routed through the bus, asserting equivalent output to the legacy direct path.

**Why this matters (architectural):** This is the first concrete step of T-capability-extraction-from-igor. Once inference routes through the bus, debugging inference happens in isolation (one device, observable via comms:// traffic), not entangled with Igor's cognition. Future agents (CC, Discord bot, swarm peers) reuse the same inference device through the same bus.
